channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: India's CCTV Ban Boosts Domestic Tech, Highlights Chip Hurdles
item.description: India's ban on non-certified foreign CCTV cameras from April 1, 2026, is changing the surveillance market. It aims to boost domestic players to 80% share but reveals India's reliance on global chip supplies. Makers face price hikes and a 3-5 year wait for local chip production, driving government efforts to create a self-reliant electronics industry.

item.title: India's ANRF Eyes Social Stock Exchange for Research Funding Boost
item.description: India's Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF) will list on the Social Stock Exchange (SSE) to attract Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) funds and diaspora contributions, diversifying beyond government budgets. ANRF aims to build a sustainable financial model for scientific research and seeks broader capital access for its mission-oriented initiatives.

item.title: India's PNG Gas Adoption Surges Amid LPG Fears, Eyes 2030 Goal
item.description: India's Piped Natural Gas (PNG) saw a major surge in March 2026, with over 3.5 lakh new connections added. The PNG Drive 2.0 campaign was extended to June amid rising concerns over LPG supply stability due to West Asia conflicts. The government is pushing this expansion to strengthen energy security and reach its goal of making natural gas 15% of India's energy mix by 2030. While domestic LPG supply is steady, the focus is now on building PNG infrastructure and encouraging consumers to switch.

item.title: India Nears Iranian Crude Delivery Under Waiver, Payment Hurdles Persist
item.description: The tanker Ping Shun is en route to India's Vadinar port with Iranian crude, a first since 2019, following a temporary US sanctions waiver aimed at easing global oil supply. Despite this opening, complex payment mechanisms due to Iran's SWIFT exclusion remain a substantial obstacle. This event unfolds as India pursues energy diversification amidst volatile geopolitical conditions and disruptions to the Strait of Hormuz, though China continues to dominate Iranian oil imports.

item.title: India Retirement: Rs 1 Cr A Mirage; Experts Push Rs 6 Cr+
item.description: Common retirement goals of Rs 1 crore are no longer sufficient for urban Indians, failing to account for persistent inflation and rising medical costs. Experts now advocate for corpus goals of Rs 6-10 crore, driven by longer lifespans and increasing expenses. This requires rethinking safe withdrawal rates and establishing dedicated healthcare funds.

item.title: Nomadic AI Closes $8.4M Seed to Unlock Physical AI Data
item.description: Nomadic AI has closed an $8.4 million seed funding round, valuing the startup at $50 million post-money. The company leverages vision-language models to convert vast amounts of autonomous machine video data into structured, searchable datasets. This enables fleet operators to identify critical 'edge cases' and accelerates reinforcement learning, positioning Nomadic as a key enabler for the physical AI revolution by providing essential data reasoning capabilities.

item.title: RBI Hits Record Dollar Forwards Amid Rupee Weakness
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) expanded its net dollar position in the rupee forward market to a record $77.25 billion by end-February. This strategy, mainly using longer-term contracts, aims to manage currency volatility. However, the Indian Rupee is facing strong pressure from Middle East conflict, rising oil prices, and significant foreign investor withdrawals, pushing the currency to new lows. The RBI is also using new rules like bank position limits, but experts question if these can overcome lasting economic challenges.

item.title: India's Record 219 Tax Deals Cut Disputes, Boost Investment Certainty
item.description: India finalized a record 219 Advance Pricing Agreements (APAs) in FY 2025-26, a major step toward tax certainty. The CBDT's move aims to cut transfer pricing disputes, unlock capital, and boost ease of doing business for multinational companies. This rise in APAs and bilateral agreements signals India's commitment to a stable investment climate.

item.title: India Removes Courier Export Cap to Boost E-commerce
item.description: India's Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) has enacted sweeping reforms, effective April 1, 2026, removing the ₹10 lakh per-consignment cap on courier exports and introducing a simplified returns framework. These changes aim to slash logistics costs and bolster global competitiveness for MSMEs and e-commerce brands, aligning with India's ambition to capture a significant share of the burgeoning global digital trade market.

item.title: India's WDFC Fully Complete, Set to Slash Logistics Costs
item.description: The Western Dedicated Freight Corridor (WDFC) has reached full completion, marking a significant infrastructure milestone. This dual-line, electrified corridor, spanning 1,506 km from Jawaharlal Nehru Port Terminal (JNPT) to Dadri, is set to revolutionize India's freight movement. Expected to lower national logistics costs from 14% to an estimated 8-9% of GDP, the project signifies a critical step towards enhanced industrial competitiveness and economic growth. The completion integrates seamlessly with the Eastern Dedicated Freight Corridor (EDFC), forming a formidable network dedicated solely to freight transport.

item.title: Gujarat Victory Forgings Files IPO Papers Amid Weak Market Conditions
item.description: Gujarat Victory Forgings Ltd has filed draft papers with SEBI for its Initial Public Offering (IPO), comprising a fresh issue of 65 lakh shares and an offer for sale of 1.32 crore shares. The company plans to use proceeds for expanding its copper cathode manufacturing capacity, repaying debt, and for general corporate needs. This move occurs as the broader Indian IPO market in FY26 shows significant weakness, with a majority of recent listings trading below their issue price and overall IPO returns turning negative.

item.title: Bitfarms Bets on AI, Sells Bitcoin as 2025 Loss Widens
item.description: Bitfarms is selling its Bitcoin to fund a strategic shift into AI infrastructure, planning to divest all crypto holdings. The company reported $229 million in revenue for 2025 but a $209 million net loss, signaling operational challenges. Bitfarms plans to rebrand as Keel Infrastructure (KEEL) while navigating a competitive AI market and energy cost pressures.

item.title: Utkarsh SFB Sells ₹1,491 Cr Bad Loans at 13% Amid Deep Losses
item.description: Utkarsh Small Finance Bank has completed the sale of a ₹1,491 crore portfolio of stressed loans for ₹195 crore, recovering only 13% of the outstanding value. This move comes as the bank grapples with a significant net loss of ₹375 crore in the last reported quarter and a gross non-performing asset ratio of 11%, substantially higher than the banking industry average. The sale, which involved two pools of microfinance loans, aims to de-risk the balance sheet but underscores the deep distress within its loan book.

item.title: Mindspace REIT Acquires ₹2,541 Cr Chennai IT Park, Boosts Portfolio
item.description: Mindspace Business Parks REIT is buying the 2.6 million sq ft Commerzone Pallikaranai IT park in Chennai for ₹2,541 crore. The deal will add to the REIT's 41.6 million sq ft portfolio and boost its Chennai assets to 9% of the total. The acquisition includes space under construction, anchored by Shell, and will be partly funded by a ₹675 crore preferential unit issuance, tapping into Chennai's strong office market.

item.title: India's Solar Module Boom Faces Cell Shortage, Imports Soar
item.description: India's solar sector is set to have 210 GW of module capacity by 2025, but only 27 GW of cell production. This forces massive imports, primarily cells, threatening cost competitiveness and supply chain strength.

item.title: India IPOs Hit Record Highs While Investor Returns Decline
item.description: India's mainboard IPO market concluded FY26 with a record ₹1.79 lakh crore raised across 112 offerings, a 10% increase over the previous year, marking two consecutive years of all-time high fundraising. This surge is juxtaposed by a significant decline in investor returns, with average listing gains hitting multi-year lows and 71 out of 108 IPOs trading below their issue price. Investor caution is evident in reduced retail applications and a dip in subscription multiples, driven by geopolitical uncertainties and a volatile secondary market.

item.title: Cargill India Reports Double-Digit Growth Driven by B2B
item.description: Cargill India is seeing strong double-digit growth, nearing the mid-teens, with its business-to-business (B2B) segment leading the way. The company uses its Innovation Centre to co-create solutions for Indian food makers, meeting demand for healthier, premium options. Key B2B growth areas include healthier foods, bakery, and confectionery.

item.title: RBI Delays Capital Rules; Brokers Get Temporary Relief, Uncertainty Lingers
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has pushed back new capital market rules by three months to July 1. This offers domestic stockbrokers temporary relief from stricter regulations on proprietary trading and lending against securities. However, uncertainty remains regarding the final impact of these rules.

item.title: Bombay HC Order on GST Tax Credits Risks India Infrastructure Funding
item.description: The Bombay High Court granted interim protection to Gateway Terminals India Pvt. Ltd. from tax recovery, highlighting a key Goods and Services Tax (GST) issue. The dispute involves tax authorities taxing transactions as services while denying input tax credit (ITC). This creates uncertainty and potential cost hikes for India's infrastructure and logistics sectors, threatening the predictability needed for project financing and investor confidence.

item.title: Rentomojo Files IPO, Bets on Rental Model for Stable Growth
item.description: Rentomojo has filed for an Initial Public Offering (IPO), planning to raise capital through a ₹150 crore fresh issue and an offer-for-sale. The company is counting on its profitable, subscription-based rental model for furniture and appliances, which offers predictable revenue and resilience in demand. This is important as the Indian IPO market faces difficulties in FY26, with many new stocks trading below their issue price due to investor caution and global uncertainty. Rentomojo's strong financial results, including a significant profit jump in the first half of FY26, position it as a promising growth prospect.

item.title: India's Low Fuel Reserves Signal Energy Risk Amid Global Volatility
item.description: India's liquid fuel reserves can cover demand for just 20 to 40 days, highlighting a significant energy security concern amid global instability. The government is monitoring the West Asia crisis and diversifying crude oil sourcing beyond traditional suppliers to include Russia, Venezuela, and exploring options in Africa and the Americas. Efforts are underway to expand strategic petroleum reserves and boost alternative fuels. However, the nation's heavy reliance on imports and limited immediate reserves pose risks to inflation, the current account deficit, and economic growth.

item.title: SEBI Reverses Ban on Retirement, Children's Funds, Tightens AMC Rules
item.description: India's securities regulator, SEBI, has reversed its plan to phase out Retirement and Children's funds, allowing these popular 'solution-oriented' schemes to continue after industry feedback. The decision comes as SEBI introduces a major overhaul of mutual fund rules set to take effect April 1, 2026. These changes include a new expense structure, tighter rules on fund portfolio overlaps, and revised fund categories, aiming to boost product variety and efficiency for Asset Management Companies (AMCs).

item.title: India Extends RoDTEP Scheme 6 Months; Exporters Seek Stability
item.description: The Indian government has extended the Remission of Duties and Taxes on Exported Products (RoDTEP) scheme for six months until September 30, 2026, to counter high freight costs and West Asia crisis disruptions. While offering immediate relief, the short duration fuels exporter frustration over policy instability, potentially hindering long-term competitiveness and the achievement of ambitious export targets. This reactive measure contrasts with the stable, predictable support seen from international competitors, raising concerns about India's ability to capitalize on trade opportunities.

item.title: Indian Real Estate: Sales Volume Falls, Prices Climb as Developers Diverge
item.description: India's housing market saw sales volume drop 0-3% in FY26, mainly due to high prices and slow approvals. New project launches also fell. Despite this, developers remain optimistic, reporting strong cash flows and seeing prices climb 6-8%. This points to a divided market with steady demand for premium homes.

item.title: India's Tax Clarity Boosts IT Exports Amidst Global Headwinds
item.description: India's Finance Act 2026 removes a long-standing tax rule that confused services to foreign clients, now clearly classifying them as exports. This change allows exporters to claim tax exemptions and refunds, boosting cash flow and cutting legal disputes. While it enhances India's position as a service hub, the sector still faces global economic uncertainty and evolving international tax rules.

item.title: Early Loan Payoff: No Fees from 2026, Credit Score Safe
item.description: Starting January 1, 2026, individuals can pay off floating-rate loans early without facing penalties. This new rule means closing loans ahead of time generally won't harm your credit score, as long as your payments have always been on time. Your credit history will reflect your past payment behavior, not just when you close the loan.

item.title: Buffett Flags Inflation, System Risks; Energy Stocks Jump on Geopolitics
item.description: Warren Buffett is warning about rising inflation and hidden risks in the financial system, noting that calm markets may hide underlying fragility. He emphasizes holding cash for safety. Meanwhile, geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are driving up oil prices and boosting energy stocks like Chevron (CVX) and Occidental Petroleum (OXY). Buffett is also reviewing donations to the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation due to recent information.

item.title: Svatantra Microfin IPO Faces Cooling Market, Regulatory Hurdles
item.description: Svatantra Microfin is preparing for its Initial Public Offering (IPO) to raise Rs 2,000 crore to Rs 3,000 crore, hiring Axis Capital and Kotak Mahindra Capital as advisors. The company shows strong growth, with Rs 3,210 crore revenue in FY25 and a large loan portfolio. However, the IPO launch faces a tough Indian market where recent listings have struggled, rising regulatory scrutiny for microfinance firms, and worries about borrowers being over-indebted. Svatantra must navigate these difficulties to get good terms.

item.title: India Auto Sector's FY27 Growth Hit by Supply Chain Shocks, Weak Demand
item.description: India's auto sector faces a challenging FY27. A strong demand recovery in late FY26, driven by GST cuts, is now threatened by supply chain issues, especially from the West Asia crisis impacting parts suppliers. Despite robust bookings, production risks and higher costs point to slower growth, leading analysts to lower forecasts.

item.title: Sundaram Clayton Stock Falls on Venu Srinivasan's Return, Governance Upset
item.description: Sundaram Clayton is experiencing acute leadership volatility following Venu Srinivasan's return as CMD and a startling reversal on the Company Secretary's departure. This upheaval coincides with widening quarterly losses and a steep stock price drop, signaling deep investor unease and governance concerns that overshadow positive sector trends.

item.title: EU Carbon Tax CBAM: India's Steel SMEs Face Costly Green Shift
item.description: The European Union's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM), set to take full effect in January 2026, presents a significant challenge for India's steel Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs). While larger players are better positioned to adapt, SMEs face disproportionately higher compliance costs and limited access to finance for decarbonization, risking exclusion from critical export supply chains.

item.title: Bengaluru Housing: Supply Surge Fuels Luxury, Squeezes Affordability
item.description: Bengaluru's housing market is experiencing a significant supply boom, but the new homes are overwhelmingly priced above the affordability benchmark. Developers are prioritizing luxury and high-end properties, delivering five high-priced units for every one priced below ₹80 lakh since 2022. This trend, coupled with a substantial portion of under-construction units, exacerbates rental burdens and pushes essential workers to the city's periphery with inadequate infrastructure. The crisis threatens Bengaluru's long-term economic competitiveness.

item.title: RBI Extends 450-Day Export Credit Amid Shipping Disruptions
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India has extended its enhanced export credit period to 450 days for both pre-shipment and post-shipment finance, valid until June 30, 2026. This support helps exporters manage debt amid ongoing logistical problems from the West Asia crisis, highlighting pressures on India's trade sector and its lenders.

item.title: CBIC Removes Courier Cap for E-Commerce Exports, Aiding Small Businesses
item.description: Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) has launched significant trade reforms effective April 1, 2026, to energize e-commerce exports. The key change removes the Rs 10 lakh cap on courier consignments, empowering MSMEs, startups, and artisans to ship higher-value goods globally. Simplified procedures for returns and risk-based checks are also introduced to reduce delays and boost competitiveness.

item.title: Paint Prices Rise Again as Crude Costs Climb, Competition Pressures Demand
item.description: Paint companies are raising prices again, driven by rising crude oil costs and geopolitical tensions. Berger Paints joins Asian Paints and Dulux in implementing price hikes to protect their profit margins. However, this strategy faces challenges: volatile commodity prices, new players like Birla Opus and JSW Paints entering aggressively, and questions about whether consumers will accept higher prices in the strong home improvement sector.

item.title: Navi MF Boosts Active Equity Funds to Seek Alpha Amid Passive Surge
item.description: Navi Mutual Fund is strategically accelerating its focus on active equity schemes, moving beyond its established passive products. CEO Aditya Mulki aims to capture alpha opportunities and higher investor inflows prevalent in active funds, despite the ongoing passive investment surge. This pivot targets differentiation in a crowded market, leveraging enhanced fund management capabilities to stand out against competitors.

item.title: India Inc. Faces Energy Price Pain: Calls for Relief Mount
item.description: India Inc. is feeling the squeeze from rising West Asia energy prices. Industry groups are asking the government for targeted relief and quicker policy action. The price surge is hitting logistics and small businesses' cash flow, but also boosting efforts for domestic self-reliance, especially in defense. Economists predict slower GDP growth and higher inflation, requiring careful management of government spending and support.

item.title: Chainalysis Uses AI Agents to Simplify Crypto Investigations
item.description: Chainalysis is launching AI agents this summer to make crypto investigations easier for law enforcement and financial firms. These tools will simplify complex analysis by using data from over 10 million past cases, democratizing access to crucial intelligence as the digital asset space faces increasing regulatory scrutiny and sophisticated threats.

item.title: India's Govt Bonds See FPI Outflows in FY26 Amid Rising Global Risks
item.description: Foreign investors sharply reversed course on Indian government bonds in FY26, moving from ₹1.2 trillion in inflows the previous year to ₹11 crore in outflows. This shift was driven by profit-taking, a smaller yield gap compared to US debt, rising crude oil prices, a weaker rupee, and worsening global instability.

item.title: Coinbase Base Targets 'Everything App' Status With Tokenized Assets, AI Payments
item.description: Coinbase's Base network is setting an ambitious 2026 goal to become a global 'everything app.' The plan includes adding tokenized assets like stocks and commodities, using AI to improve stablecoin payments, and supporting developers. This strategy aims to connect traditional finance with the digital economy as the Ethereum network evolves.
